# Layout Patterns

Pick the layout that matches the argument the diagram needs to make.

## Pipeline

Use for sequential processing.

- Arrange left to right
- Keep stages evenly spaced
- Put artifacts above or below the stage they explain

## Fan-out

Use when one source branches into several outputs or consumers.

- Keep the source visually central
- Angle branches so they are easy to trace
- Avoid symmetric clutter if one branch is more important than the others

## Convergence

Use when multiple inputs combine into one system or decision.

- Show inputs clearly separated before they merge
- Make the merge point visually stronger than the inputs

## Lifecycle

Use for state transitions or long-running processes.

- Prefer a horizontal timeline or a clockwise cycle
- Make retries, waits, and terminal states visibly distinct

## Layered architecture

Use for platform or system architecture.

- Stack layers vertically
- Keep cross-layer arrows intentional and limited
- Show trust or boundary zones with larger background containers

## Side-by-side comparison

Use for before/after or option comparisons.

- Mirror structure between columns
- Highlight the deciding differences only
- Keep labels aligned for fast scanning
